payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claiming earned pocket money for completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG915 establishes the claiming process as the core operational purpose of the enterprise at the strategic level.

payload.description: Defining task-reward relationships
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates the parental process of establishing which tasks yield which rewards as strategically important.

payload.description: Verifying completed tasks and earned amounts
payload.justification: Signal SG919 defines verification as a strategic oversight process performed by parents to ensure system integrity.

payload.description: Fulfilling payment of earned pocket money
payload.justification: Signal SG919 establishes payment fulfillment as a core parental process required to complete the value chain.

payload.description: Pocket money as earned compensation for task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 establishes pocket money as a core strategic entity earned through task completion, clearly articulating what is of importance to the enterprise.

payload.description: Task performed by children
payload.justification: Signals SG915 and SG916 both reference tasks as the fundamental work unit that drives the pocket money system, making it a strategically important entity.

payload.description: Task-reward relationship linking children's work to compensation
payload.justification: Signal SG916 explicitly articulates the need to define task-reward relationships, representing a strategic relationship between work and compensation entities.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: Signal SG918 identifies weekly earnings as a strategically important data entity for motivational transparency purposes.

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle for pocket money reporting
payload.justification: Signal SG918 identifies weekly as the strategic time cycle for earnings transparency and reporting to children.

payload.description: Child earning and claiming pocket money
payload.justification: Signals SG915, SG917, and SG918 consistently identify children as primary actors who perform tasks, claim earnings, and require visibility and transparency.

payload.description: Parent defining tasks and overseeing payments
payload.justification: Signals SG916 and SG919 establish parents as strategic actors responsible for defining work opportunities and ensuring payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money
payload.justification: Signal SG915 directly establishes this as the core purpose and strategic goal of the enterprise.

payload.description: Provide visibility of available opportunities and completed achievements to children
payload.justification: Signal SG917 articulates the strategic goal of transparency for children regarding both prospective and retrospective work activity.

payload.description: Motivate children through weekly earnings transparency
payload.justification: Signal SG918 defines motivational transparency as a strategic goal driving the system's design for children.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight for verification and payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal SG919 establishes parental control and accountability as a strategic goal ensuring system integrity and fairness.

payload.description: Parent reviews child earnings and task completion for payment distribution
payload.justification: SG926 defines the parental reporting capability for reviewing child earnings and task completion to support payment distribution, representing a review and decision-making process.

payload.description: Parent specifies tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG922 defines the business capability for parents to specify tasks with associated monetary values, representing a core business process.

payload.description: Child claims completion of pocket money task
payload.justification: SG921 establishes the core business concept of children claiming completion of pocket money tasks, representing a fundamental business process.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG924 defines the business capability for children to view both available and completed tasks within the current week.

payload.description: System generates weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG925 specifies the weekly summary capability showing total earnings aggregated for each child, representing a summarization process.

payload.description: Task availability resets at the start of each new week
payload.justification: SG927 establishes the business rule for weekly task availability reset, defining a governing rule for task lifecycle management.

payload.description: Past task completion data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: SG928 specifies the business requirement to retain and provide access to past task completion data, representing a data retention business rule.

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG922 explicitly defines the business capability for parents to specify tasks with associated monetary values, clearly identifying Task as a core business entity with a monetary attribute.

payload.description: Child actor performing tasks
payload.justification: SG923 specifies the relationship between completed tasks and child actors; SG925 aggregates earnings for each child, establishing Child as a distinct business entity.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary aggregated by child
payload.justification: SG925 defines a weekly summary showing total earnings per child, indicating a derived or aggregated data entity representing accumulated task value.

payload.description: Historical task completion data
payload.justification: SG928 specifies retention and access to past task completion data, establishing historical records as a persistent business entity.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: SG923 references completed tasks and SG924 distinguishes available versus completed tasks, indicating completion status as a key attribute of the Task entity.

payload.description: Task monetary value
payload.justification: SG922 explicitly defines tasks with associated monetary values, establishing monetary value as a fundamental attribute of Task.

payload.description: Child claims completion of task
payload.justification: SG921 establishes children claiming completion of tasks; SG923 specifies the relationship between completed tasks and the child who performed them.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money task cycle
payload.justification: SG921 establishes weekly cycle for claiming completion; SG924 references current week; SG925 specifies weekly summary; SG927 defines weekly reset.

payload.description: Start of new week triggering task availability reset
payload.justification: SG927 establishes the weekly task availability reset at the start of each new week, identifying the new week start as a business event.

payload.description: Child performing and claiming tasks
payload.justification: SG921 establishes children claiming completion; SG923 identifies child as the actor performing tasks; SG924 defines child viewing capability.

payload.description: Parent specifying tasks and reviewing completion
payload.justification: SG922 defines parent capability to specify tasks; SG926 defines parental reporting and payment distribution responsibility.

payload.description: Support parent payment distribution based on child task completion
payload.justification: SG926 explicitly states that the reporting capability supports payment distribution, identifying payment distribution as a business goal.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must remain accessible
payload.justification: SG928 specifies the requirement to retain and provide access to past data, representing a business constraint on data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation of earned amounts
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic specifying weekly calculation represents a logical function that computes earned amounts.

payload.description: Task availability reset at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: State transition logic defining reset behavior represents a logical process that executes at specified intervals.

payload.description: Historical task records must be retained persistently
payload.justification: Data retention requirement explicitly stated as a rule governing how historical data must be managed.

payload.description: Task completion event associates to child entity
payload.justification: Signals describe association logic between task completion events and child entities, representing a logical relationship in the data model.

payload.description: Historical task record
payload.justification: Data retention rule implies the existence of a logical entity representing historical task records that must be persisted.

payload.description: Task completion event occurrence
payload.justification: References to task completion events indicate a logical event that triggers association logic.

payload.description: Weekly temporal cycle for task system operations
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly boundaries and weekly calculations, establishing a recurring temporal cycle governing system behavior.

payload.description: Weekly boundary reached triggers task availability reset
payload.justification: State transition logic at weekly boundaries represents a temporal trigger condition that initiates the reset process.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for deployment targets
payload.justification: Both signals describe platform compatibility constraints specifying required OS versions. This represents a technical constraint on deployment at the Builder level.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Child Economic Participation
payload.description: The enterprise concern for how children engage as economic participants through work-based compensation relationships, including their entitlements to claim earned value and visibility into earning opportunities.

payload.name: Work-Compensation Governance
payload.description: The enterprise accountability for establishing and maintaining the formal relationship between work performed and compensation earned, including the definition of valid task-reward pairings and the structure of compensable activities.

payload.name: Payment Obligation Fulfillment
payload.description: The enterprise commitment to verify earned compensation claims and discharge payment obligations to participants, ensuring accountability for amounts owed based on completed work.

payload.name: Performance Transparency
payload.description: The enterprise responsibility to provide periodic reporting and visibility of earnings activity, enabling participants to understand their accumulated compensation and stakeholders to exercise oversight.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the scope and terms of remunerative work obligations available to child participants, including their monetary value and availability cycles.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for receiving, recording, and maintaining completion claims made by child participants against defined work obligations, ensuring verifiable status throughout the participation lifecycle.

payload.name: Settlement Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for evaluating earned entitlements, producing periodic settlement summaries, and enabling payment distribution decisions based on verified completion records and established values.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving and ensuring ongoing accessibility of completion activity records beyond active settlement cycles, supporting accountability and retrospective review obligations.

payload.name: Task Completion Event Model
payload.description: Logical model governing task completion events, their structural associations to child entities, and the triggers that initiate completion processing within the task lifecycle.

payload.name: Task Historical Record Persistence
payload.description: Logical structure and constraints ensuring completed task records are retained persistently, maintaining historical integrity and supporting temporal data requirements.

payload.name: Weekly Task Cycle Management
payload.description: Logical temporal model defining weekly operational cycles, task availability refresh behavior at cycle boundaries, and the triggering logic that governs periodic task system state transitions.

payload.name: Earnings Derivation Logic
payload.description: Logical process model for deriving aggregated earned amounts over weekly temporal boundaries, transforming task completion data into summarized earnings values.

payload.name: Platform Version Compatibility
payload.description: Physical operating system version requirements and constraints that govern which deployment targets and runtime environments can host the system components.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise pocket money as earned compensation for task completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the foundational economic relationship between tasks and compensation at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Combines the claiming process, the child actor's entitlement, and the enabling goal into a single enterprise commitment to provide this capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility of available tasks and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to transparency regarding earning opportunities and achievements, supporting informed child participation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks as a category of work performed by children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise-level recognition of 'task' as the fundamental work entity associated with children, providing the structural foundation for the work-compensation relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the relationship between tasks and rewards as the basis for children's compensation.
payload.rationale: Declares the enterprise accountability for the structural association linking children's work (tasks) to their compensation (rewards), forming the core relationship of the work-compensation domain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall define task-reward relationships.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise obligation to perform the definitional activity that establishes which tasks correspond to which rewards, operationalising the structural relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall verify completed tasks and earned amounts.
payload.rationale: Process CCI in How column expressing enterprise obligation to verify completion and compensation before payment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall fulfill payment of earned pocket money.
payload.rationale: Process CCI in How column expressing enterprise commitment to discharge payment obligations for verified earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight for verification and payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal CCI requiring governance mechanism; consolidated with Who-column actor CCI identifying the parent as the oversight party. Both express the single enterprise constraint that parental oversight must be enabled for verification and payment activities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide a weekly earnings summary.
payload.rationale: The What-column entity (weekly earnings summary) combined with the When-column cycle (weekly reporting period) establishes a single functional obligation: the enterprise commits to providing earnings summaries on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view their earnings transparency on a weekly basis.
payload.rationale: The Why-column goal (motivate children through weekly earnings transparency) expresses an enterprise commitment to enable visibility of earnings information. At Row 1, this is framed as an enablement obligation rather than system behavior.

payload.statement: The Task Stewardship capability shall maintain tasks as entities with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Refines recognition of pocket money as earned compensation by establishing the structural foundation: tasks with monetary values.
payload.fit_criteria: Each task entity shall have a defined monetary value attribute.

payload.statement: The Completion Accountability capability shall record child claims of task completion.
payload.rationale: Refines enabling children to claim earned pocket money by defining the specific process for recording completion claims.
payload.fit_criteria: Each child claim of task completion shall be recorded with child identifier and task identifier.

payload.statement: The Completion Accountability capability shall enable children to view available and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Directly refines providing children visibility of tasks by specifying the viewing capability within the Completion Accountability domain.
payload.fit_criteria: Child actors shall be able to retrieve lists of both available tasks and tasks marked as completed.

payload.statement: The Task Stewardship capability shall define tasks as work obligations with completion status tracking.
payload.rationale: Refines recognition of tasks as work by defining the structural components: task entities and their completion status attributes.
payload.fit_criteria: Task entities shall include a completion status attribute.

payload.statement: The Task Stewardship capability shall associate each task with a monetary value as the basis for child compensation.
payload.rationale: Refines establishing the task-reward relationship by defining the structural linkage between tasks and monetary values.
payload.fit_criteria: Each task shall have a monetary value attribute representing the compensation for completion.

payload.statement: The Task Stewardship capability shall enable parents to specify tasks with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Refines defining task-reward relationships by specifying the process and actor responsible for establishing them.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent actors shall be able to create task definitions including monetary value specifications.

payload.statement: The Settlement Governance capability shall enable parents to review child earnings and task completion for verification.
payload.rationale: Refines verification of completed tasks and earned amounts by defining the parental review process within Settlement Governance.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent actors shall be able to access child earnings and completed task records for verification purposes.

payload.statement: The Settlement Governance capability shall support parent payment distribution decisions based on verified completion.
payload.rationale: Refines fulfilling payment by defining the capability supporting parent payment distribution decisions.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent actors shall be able to initiate payment distribution based on reviewed completion records.

payload.statement: The Settlement Governance capability shall require parental review before payment distribution.
payload.rationale: Refines enabling parental oversight by constraining payment to require parental review within Settlement Governance.
payload.fit_criteria: Payment distribution shall only occur after parent review of earnings and task completion.

payload.statement: The Settlement Governance capability shall generate weekly earnings summaries aggregated by child.
payload.rationale: Refines providing weekly earnings summary by specifying the generation process and output entity within Settlement Governance.
payload.fit_criteria: A weekly earnings summary entity shall be generated for each child at the end of each weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The Settlement Governance capability shall enable children to view their weekly earnings summaries.
payload.rationale: Refines enabling children to view earnings transparency by specifying access to weekly earnings summary entities.
payload.fit_criteria: Child actors shall be able to retrieve their own weekly earnings summary.

payload.statement: The Task Stewardship capability shall reset task availability at the start of each new week.
payload.rationale: Refines defining task-reward relationships by specifying the temporal rule for task availability cycles.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability shall be updated at the start of each weekly cycle event.

payload.statement: The Historical Record Stewardship capability shall retain past task completion data and ensure accessibility.
payload.rationale: Refines verification by constraining the system to preserve historical completion records for ongoing verification accountability.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task completion data shall remain accessible beyond the active settlement cycle.

payload.statement: A task shall have an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural composition of a task entity—that each task carries a monetary value attribute. What-column structural assertion derived from entity and attribute CCIs.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle for task availability.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the temporal cycle governing task availability. When-column cycle CCI establishes the business obligation behind the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall reset task availability at the start of each new week.
payload.rationale: Business rule constraining when task availability resets, triggered by the start-of-week event. How-column rule CCI combined with When-column event CCI establishes the constraint condition.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables the child's claiming action. Complementary pair with the actor-subject requirement — this names what the system provides at the boundary (the capability), not how it provides it.

payload.statement: The system shall make task status visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the affordance that enables viewing. Complementary to the actor-subject viewing requirement. References the What-column attribute CCI for task completion status as the object being made visible.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completion claim.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to record completion claims. The What-column relationship CCI (child claims completion of task) implies a persistent business record of that relationship event.

payload.statement: A task shall have a completion status.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion derived from the What-column attribute CCI. Task completion status is a structural attribute of the task entity that must be recognised at the business level.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings summaries visible to entitled parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance at the boundary that enables parent review capability by providing aggregated earnings information.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings summary aggregated by child.
payload.rationale: Business-subject responsibility for the structural artefact that records each child's earned entitlements over the weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly pocket money task cycle.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule governing the temporal boundary for task completion evaluation and earnings settlement.

payload.statement: The business shall support payment distribution decisions based on verified child task completion.
payload.rationale: Business rule constraining the basis for settlement decisions to completion verification, ensuring entitlement integrity.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a historical record of task completion data.
payload.rationale: Business capability to preserve task completion records beyond active settlement cycles. The What-column CCI names the entity (historical task completion data); the How-column CCI establishes the retention obligation. This is a business responsibility performed behind the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall ensure historical task completion data remains accessible.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint on accessibility of historical data, supported by the How-column rule. This governs the ongoing availability obligation distinct from the retention obligation.

payload.statement: A child can perform and claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs describe the same actor (child) boundary-crossing capability at Row 2: the Who-column CCI explicitly names the child actor performing and claiming tasks, and the What-column CCI identifies the child actor performing tasks. These are complementary expressions of a single actor-action obligation at the system boundary. The statement uses ACTOR subject form (boundary test: the child reaches in through the system) with the child's real action verbs (perform, claim). This is a Who-column, boundary-crossing statement naming the system boundary as required by Cell Occupancy.

payload.statement: The system shall comply with the minimum operating system version constraints specified for each deployment target platform.
payload.rationale: Operating system version compatibility establishes a normative boundary condition that governs which runtime environments can host the system components. Derived from Why-column physical constraint governing deployment targets.
